title: Custom delegate declaration + subscriber (distinct from system event handlers)
instruction: In the sandbox model, create: (1) a class DemoNoteSubject declaring a custom delegate, delegate void noteAdded(str _noteId) {} (empty-body method entry, the X++ delegate-declaration convention -- verified against a real shipped delegate), and a method addNote(str _noteId) that fires it via this.noteAdded(_noteId); (2) a class DemoNoteSubjectEventHandler with a static method noteAdded(str _noteId) decorated [SubscribesTo(classStr(ConDemoNoteSubject), delegateStr(ConDemoNoteSubject, noteAdded))] subscribing to that delegate (a no-op body is fine -- this case exercises the delegate DECLARATION + SUBSCRIPTION wiring, not a business side effect). This is a DIFFERENT extensibility mechanism from L2-event-handler-basic's system [DataEventHandler] table events -- a hand-declared class delegate, not a platform event. Both classes must build clean.
